draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer organic, approachable interfaces over sharp or minimal aesthetics
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward warm, welcoming, human-centered design language
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward crisp, precise, modern geometric forms

evidence.hypothesis: The user may prefer organic, approachable interfaces over sharp or minimal aesthetics
evidence.implication: Lean toward warm, welcoming, human-centered design language

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer gentle, approachable interface geometry over sharp edges
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design with curved UI elements, smooth button corners, flowing shapes
facade.rejectImplies: Design with angular corners, sharp edges, geometric precision

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer friendly, conversational language over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design voice should feel like talking to a colleague—warm, accessible, maybe a touch playful. Reduce corporate jargon.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want confident, authoritative tone. Professional polish matters more than approachability. Keep distance between tool and user.

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer fluid, continuous motion over instant state changes when navigating between tools or switching contexts.
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace eased transitions, crossfades, and animated state changes. Motion should feel like sliding into the next space, not teleporting.
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize instant feedback and zero-latency interactions. Users want to see results immediately without waiting for animations to complete.

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer quantified data in structured, scannable formats over flowing prose
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data presentation vs narrative explanation
facade.label: Numbers in boxes
facade.acceptImplies: Build dashboards with metric cards, KPIs, and grid-based layouts; minimize prose descriptions
facade.rejectImplies: Emphasize conversational explanations, narrative flow, and contextual storytelling; reduce tabular/metric density

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar navigation combined with vertically stacked tab-like sections reduces cognitive load in complex workspaces by keeping navigation visible and groupable.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (persistent vs. context-dependent) and information density (grouped vs. flat)
facade.label: Sidebar with vertical tabs
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers contained, modular workspace organization where tool categories are always accessible without modal switching.
facade.rejectImplies: User favors flatter hierarchies, horizontal tab bars, or dynamic contextual navigation that adapts based on active task rather than fixed structure.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer restrained, focused interfaces that reduce visual noise and support deep work concentration
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs Digital (leaning into digital precision and clarity)
facade.label: Monochrome and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Design the workspace with a limited color palette, generous whitespace, and functional typography—emphasizing clarity over warmth
facade.rejectImplies: The user likely values visual richness, color, and personality in their workspace—move toward layered details and chromatic depth

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
